title: LLM Engineer / AI Engineer
experience_text: 5 - 8 Years (Minimum 5 years as a LLM/ AI engineer)
description_snippet: Role:LLM Engineer / AI Engineer  * Location: Rai Durg, Hyderabad * Work mode- Hybrid model working (3 days work from office) * Experience: 5 - 8 Years (Minimum 5 years as a LLM/ AI engineer) * Mandatory Skills: RAG Archiecture, LLM, Open AI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral, RAG Architecture, Version Tunning, DevOps * Budget: 18 - 32 LPA * Qualification: Bachelor of Engineering - Bachelor of Technology (B.E./B.Tech.) * Notice period: Immediate / early joiners (Max. 15-30 days) * Interview Process: 2 - 3 Technical rounds Important Note  * We are currently prioritizing immediate / early joiners (maximum 15-30 days- notice period above 30 days will be automatically rejected.). * All mandatory technical skills must be clearly highlighted within the project descriptions in your resume, not just listed in the Skills or Roles & Responsibilities sections . Position Overview  We are seeking a highly technical LLM / AI Engineer to build, optimize, and deploy the models that power our Agentic AI initiatives. You will be the primary person responsible for ensuring our agents don't just "chat," but execute with precision. Your focus will be on model selection, prompt engineering at scale, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) optimization, and ensuring our AI output is grounded in our specific business logic. This role requires a "builder" mentality, someone who can take a raw model and turn it into a production-ready asset that significantly shortens our support cycles Key Responsibilities  * Model Engineering: Implement and optimize LLM pipelines using both proprietary (OpenAI, Anthropic) and open-source models (Llama, Mistral) to find the best cost-to-performance ratio. * RAG Architecture: Build and maintain sophisticated RAG systems to ensure agents have real-time access to our internal documentation, customer history, and trading platform data. * Prompt Engineering & Tuning: Develop and version-control complex prompt templates, utilizing techniques like Few-Shot prompting and Chain-of-Thought to improve reasoning. * Evaluation & Testing: Establish "LLM-as-a-judge" frameworks and automated testing to measure hallucination rates, toxicity, and accuracy before agents hit the non-production environments. * Tool-Use Logic: Program the logic that allows agents to use external tools (APIs, Database queries, Moltbot functions) reliably and safely. * Latency Optimization: Streamline model inference and data retrieval to ensure the "Super Employee" responds in near real-time, improving customer satisfaction. * DevOps for AI: Familiarity with LLMOps tools for monitoring model performance and drift in production. Additional Qualifications  * Experience in high-throughput environments (e.g., Trading, FinTech, or Global Logistics). * Previous experience building and deploying autonomous agents in a production environment. * Familiarity with Claude and OpenClaw ecosystem. Why Join ?  * Opportunity to lead transformative initiatives, modernizing legacy systems and shaping the future of trading technology. * Work with cutting-edge technologies in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. * Competitive compensation, professional growth opportunities, and the chance to work with industry-leading experts. Skills: ai engineer,llm,rag archiecture,devops

description_snippet: Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward. BigQuery is the leader in the enterprise cloud data warehouse market. It is a high growth product area and plays a key role in contributing to GCP’s overall business and growth. BigQuery AI/ML is on a mission to democratize AI and ML. It takes away all the complexity and challenge of doing AI and ML and reduces it down to a few lines of SQL statements to train and predict machine learning models. Over the past few years, BQML has grown into a powerful AI and ML platform, growing rapidly in terms of usage and is a key differentiator for BigQuery. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work with BigQuery, Vertex AI, and other GCP services to help elevate BigQuery to the next level. Next steps Step 1. Visit our website. Step 2. Click 'Talk to Jack'. Step 3. Talk to Jack so he can understand your experience and ambitions. Step 4. If Jill thinks you're a great fit and her client wants to meet you, they will make the introduction. Step 6. If not, Jack will find you excellent alternatives. All for free. We never post fake jobs This isn't a trick. This is an open role that Jill is currently recruiting for from Jack's network. Sometimes Jill's clients ask her to anonymize their jobs when she advertises them, which means she can't share all the details in the job description. We appreciate this can make them look a bit suspect, but there isn't much we can do about it. Give Jack a spin! You could land this role. title: Software Engineer( Agentic AI Cloud Migration)
description_snippet: Job ID: 207014 Required Travel :Minimal Location: :Pune Who are we Amdocs helps those who build the future to make it amazing. With our market-leading portfolio of software products and services, we unlock our customers innovative potential, empowering them to provide next-generation communication and media experiences for both the individual end user and enterprise customers. Our employees around the globe are here to accelerate service providers migration to the cloud, enable them to differentiate in the 5G era, and digitalize and automate their operations. Listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market, Amdocs had revenue of $5.00 billion in fiscal 2024. For more information, visit _www.amdocs.com_ At Amdocs, our mission is to empower our employees to Live Amazing, Do Amazing every day. We believe in creating a workplace where you not only excel professionally but also thrive personally. Through our culture of making a real impact, fostering growth, embracing flexibility, and building connections, we enable them to live meaningful lives while making a difference in the world. In one sentence As a Senior Agentic Software Engineer Cloud Migration, you will design and build AI agents that automate key steps of cloud migration programs: discovery, assessment, migration planning, wave design, and infrastructure-as-code generation. You'll combine your cloud/infra expertise with GenAI and multi-agent orchestration to create reusable, platform-agnostic accelerators used across Amdocs Cloud Studio engagements. You'll work in a cross-functional agentic squad with architects, data engineers, and the R&D foundation team to turn real customer migration patterns (e.g., VMware exit, OpenShift, hybrid cloud) into enterprise-grade agentic solutions. What will your job look like Key Responsibilities Design and implement LLM-powered agents that support: o Environment discovery and inventory analysis o Workload classification (6Rs), grouping, and dependency mapping o Migration wave planning and landing zone recommendations o IaC template generation (Terraform/CloudFormation) Collaborate with the foundation/platform team to: o Use shared memory, context, and orchestration frameworks o Contribute new agentic primitives (e.g., workload analyzer, IaC generator) for reuse Integrate agents into cloud-native services (APIs, microservices, or workflows on Kubernetes/serverless). Implement guardrails, evaluation, and observability for AI behavior (metrics, logging, test harnesses). Partner with Cloud Studio architects and delivery teams to turn field patterns into reusable accelerators. Contribute to demo environments and portals (e.g., self-service migration demos for presales and events). Document designs, decisions, and patterns in a way that others can reuse and extend. Common Skills & Experience (All Agentic Engineer Roles) GenAI & Agentic Systems Hands-on experience integrating LLMs via APIs (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI / Bedrock / others). Practical experience with at least one framework such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, or similar for building LLM/agentic apps. Strong prompt & context engineering skills: structuring system prompts, chain-of-thought hints, retrieval contexts, and examples. Experience building tool-using agents (e.g., agents that call code analyzers, cloud APIs, or databases). Understanding of RAG patterns (retrieval-augmented generation): embeddings, vector stores, hybrid search, and when RAG vs. fine-tuning is appropriate. Exposure to multi-agent orchestration (e.g., task decomposition, agent-to-agent handoff, shared context/state machines) is a strong plus. All you need is... Software Engineering & Architecture 58+ years of hands-on software engineering experience. Strong coding skills in Python plus at least one of: TypeScript, Java, Go, or .NET. Experience building APIs, services, and CLIs, and integrating them in a cloud environment. Solid understanding of cloud-native patterns: microservices, containerization (Docker), orchestration (Kubernetes), CI/CD pipelines. Familiarity with logging, monitoring, and observability; ability to design systems that can be debugged in production. Strong testing mindset: unit tests, integration tests, plus basic understanding of LLM evaluation approaches (golden datasets, scoring, human review loops). Ways of Working Ability to work in cross-functional squads (architects, engineers, UX, PM, domain SMEs). Experience in consulting, professional services, or product engineering is a plus. Strong written and verbal communication; able to explain complex designs to technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfortable working in a fast-moving R&D environment with iterative discovery and delivery. Cloud Migration Domain-Specific Skills Cloud & Infrastructure Expertise Hands-on experience with at least two major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP). Strong understanding of on-prem & virtualized environments, particularly VMware; familiarity with migration tools/approaches (e.g., VMware exit, lift-and-shift, replatform). Proficiency with Infrastructure-as-Code: o Terraform strongly preferred o CloudFormation / Bicep / ARM/CDK is a plus Solid grasp of: o VPC/VNet design (subnets, routing, NAT, load balancers) o Identity & access management (IAM, RBAC) o Security & compliance considerations (data residency, encryption, network segmentation) Cloud Migration Concepts Familiarity with cloud migration strategies (6Rs/7Rs, wave planning, landing zones, blueprinting). Experience with: o Application and infrastructure discovery/assessment o Grouping workloads based on dependency and risk o Designing Cloud landing zones and target reference architectures Experience participating in or leading migration programs (even if non-AI) is a strong plus. Agentic Migration Use Cases Ability to build agents that can: o Read CMDB/exported inventories and identify migration candidates and blockers o Propose wave plans, target architectures, and landing zones based on patterns o Generate IaC templates, runbooks, and validation checklists o Surface risks and recommendations (unsupported OS, deprecated tech, compliance flags) Nice-to-Have Experience integrating with migration tools (AWS MGN, Azure Migrate, HCX, etc.). Exposure to FinOps or cost modeling for cloud migrations. Why you will love this job: Ability to work in cross-functional squads (architects, engineers, UX, PM, domain SMEs). Amdocs is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce

title: Junior Applied AI Builder
description_snippet: Junior Applied AI Builder Location : Remote (India) Type : Full-time or Internship Experience : 0 to 2 years Role Overview : Blue Sherpa builds AI systems for lenders and credit funds, helping risk teams compress the time between signal and action. We are hiring junior applied AI builders who like working on real problems with today\'s AI stack. This is not a traditional junior ML role, and it is not a no-code automation role. It is for people who like building real systems, writing code, debugging what breaks, and improving workflows until they become genuinely useful. The work sits at the intersection of : - Applied AI and LLM systems - Practical engineering - Data and modeling - Real business problems in credit, risk, and analytics You will work closely with experienced practitioners and take ownership of small problems end to end. What You\'ll Work On - Build LLM-powered workflows for analytical and operational tasks - Work with prompting, structured outputs, tool use, retrieval, embeddings, and related techniques - Design evals to understand where a system is failing and how to improve it - Debug issues involving model behavior, workflow logic, and messy real-world data - Build simple end-to-end pipelines, prototypes, and internal tools - Work on practical data and modeling problems where needed - Turn ambiguous business problems into clear, working technical solutions What We\'re Looking For : Strong coding ability : - You should be able to write clean, modular, debuggable code in Python. Comfort with today\'s AI stack : You should have meaningful exposure to some of the following : - LLM APIs and workflows - Prompting and structured generation - Embeddings and retrieval - Evals - Fine-tuning - Tool use or agent-like workflows You don\'t need deep expertise in everything, but you should be comfortable building with modern AI tools and understanding the tradeoffs. Builder mindset We are looking for people who : - Can build things outside formal assignments - Can show real projects and code - Can enjoy learning by doing - Can make progress independently - Would like figuring things out when the path is unclear Debugging instinct : A strong signal is that you have built something that did not work, spent time understanding why, and improved it. We value people who can explain what they tried, what broke, how they diagnosed it, and what they changed. Practical problem solving : You should be able to break down ambiguous problems, choose a reasonable first approach, and move from idea to working system without needing perfect instructions. Strong Signals : These are strong positive signals for us : - GitHub or equivalent with real projects - working AI systems you can explain clearly - projects involving LLM workflows, evals, retrieval, embeddings, fine-tuning, or end-to-end pipelines - evidence that you can build, not just complete coursework - examples of independent project work - the ability to explain what failed and how you fixed it Nice to Have : - Experience with RAG or retrieval-based workflows - Familiarity with Hugging Face, PyTorch, or TensorFlow - Experience with FastAPI, Flask, or similar frameworks - Comfort with data pipelines or cloud environments - Exposure to analytics, credit, risk, or operational use cases What This Role Is Not : - Not pure ML research - Not a generic data analyst role - Not just prompt engineering - Not just no-code automation This is a builder role for people who want to work across AI, data, and systems to solve real problems. Who Should Apply : - Students - Recent graduates - Early-career builders - People with strong project work, even if their background is unconventional.

title: Gen AI Python with AWS
description_snippet: About the Role We are looking for a mid-level AI Engineer with 23 years of experience to join our team in building next-generation Agentic AI solutions. You will be responsible for designing and deploying production-ready Generative AI systems, focusing on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and autonomous agents that can reason, plan, and execute complex workflows. This is a hands-on engineering role where you will leverage the full power of the AWS ecosystem to turn cutting-edge AI research into scalable, secure, and high-impact enterprise applications. Key Responsibilities * Agentic Orchestration: Design and implement autonomous agents using Amazon Bedrock Agents or frameworks like LangGraph and CrewAI to automate multi-step business processes. * Advanced RAG Pipelines: Build and optimize end-to-end RAG workflows, including data ingestion, chunking strategies, and hybrid search using Amazon OpenSearch Serverless. * Serverless AI Infrastructure: Develop and deploy AI-driven microservices using AWS Lambda and API Gateway , ensuring low-latency and scalable performance. * Prompt Engineering: Design, test, and iterate on complex system prompts and orchestration logic (e.g., Chain-of-Thought, ReAct) to improve model grounding and accuracy. * Model Evaluation & Safety: Implement evaluation frameworks to measure faithfulness and relevancy, and apply Bedrock Guardrails for responsible AI. * Collaboration: Work closely with architects and product owners to translate business requirements into technical blueprints and functional prototypes. Required Skills & Qualifications * Programming: High proficiency in Python (clean code, asynchronous programming, and API development with FastAPI/Flask). * AWS AI Services: Deep hands-on experience with Amazon Bedrock (Knowledge Bases, Agents, and Guardrails). * Core AWS Services: Proven experience with AWS Lambda , API Gateway , S3 , and IAM policy management. * Vector Databases: Practical knowledge of Amazon OpenSearch (or similar like Pinecone/Milvus) for semantic search and metadata filtering. * Generative AI Concepts: Solid understanding of LLM mechanics (Claude, Llama, Titan), token management, and RAG architectures. * DevOps/MLOps: Experience with CI/CD pipelines, version control (Git), and basic cloud security best practices (encryption-at-rest, VPC). Preferred (Nice-to-Have) * Experience with AWS Step Functions for complex workflow orchestration. * Knowledge of Model Context Protocol (MCP) to connect agents to external data sources. * AWS Certified AI Practitioner or Machine Learning Specialty certification. * Experience in the financial services or enterprise automation domain.
